raw_wording: Notre stratégie d’investissement reste prudente et équilibrée entre actions et obligations. Elle privilégie les petites valeurs européennes au détriment des grandes capitalisations américaines liées notamment à l'IA.
why: Selective equity stance rather than broad-beta risk taking.

raw_wording: Les petites capitalisations européennes devraient bénéficier du recentrage vers une croissance plus domestique, de l’union des marchés de capitaux mais aussi d’éventuelles baisses de taux de la BCE.
why: Europe benefits from domestic recovery, capital-markets union, and ECB easing.

raw_wording: Nous restons prudents sur les grandes capitalisations liées à l’IA (notamment les MAG7) et privilégions les petites valeurs européennes au détriment des grandes capitalisations américaines liées à l'IA.
why: Crowded US AI mega-caps are explicitly de-emphasized.

raw_wording: Les petites capitalisations européennes devraient bénéficier du recentrage vers une croissance plus domestique, de l’union des marchés de capitaux mais aussi d’éventuelles baisses de taux de la BCE.
why: European small caps are presented as under-owned recovery beneficiaries.

raw_wording: Les valeurs américaines Value ont également leur place dans cette recherche de diversification. Elles pourraient tirer parti d’un possible retour à une politique économique très expansionniste aux États-Unis.
why: US value is part of the diversification basket.

raw_wording: Nous privilégions ainsi les dettes hybrides financières et d’entreprises, essentiellement dans un univers d’émetteurs Investment Grade, ainsi que la dette émergente, qui devrait profiter de l’assouplissement monétaire de la Fed, et enfin les stratégies de portage.
why: Preference for spread and carry products rather than pure sovereign duration.

raw_wording: Nous privilégions ainsi les dettes hybrides financières et d’entreprises, essentiellement dans un univers d’émetteurs Investment Grade.
why: Investment-grade issuers are explicitly preferred.

raw_wording: Nous privilégions ainsi les dettes hybrides financières et d’entreprises, essentiellement dans un univers d’émetteurs Investment Grade.
why: Financial and corporate hybrids are favored.

raw_wording: Nous privilégions ainsi la dette émergente, qui devrait profiter de l’assouplissement monétaire de la Fed.
why: EM debt is a named beneficiary of Fed easing.

raw_wording: Les valeurs aurifères pourraient à nouveau tirer leur épingle du jeu si la recomposition au sein de la Réserve Fédérale devait se traduire par une empreinte politique plus marquée.
why: Gold-linked exposure is supported as a hedge against politicized Fed risk.

raw_wording: Nous restons prudents sur les grandes capitalisations liées à l’IA et privilégions plutôt le thème du Big Data, en particulier les entreprises utilisatrices qui pourront le mieux tirer parti de l’IA.
why: Favors AI users and Big Data while staying cautious on crowded AI leaders.

raw_wording: Le secteur de la santé répond également à ces caractéristiques. Longtemps pénalisé par des obstacles d’ordre politique, il voit désormais son horizon s’éclaircir et commence à reprendre des couleurs.
why: Healthcare is treated as a preferred and recovering theme.

raw_wording: Nous privilégions également les entreprises qui bénéficieront du thème de la résilience et de la souveraineté, aussi bien au niveau mondial qu’européen, la mise en œuvre du rapport Draghi étant l’un des objectifs prioritaires de la Commission européenne.
why: Sovereignty is an explicit preferred theme, especially in Europe.

raw_wording: Notre stratégie d’investissement privilégie les thématiques de la résilience, la santé et le Big Data.
why: Resilience and Big Data are recurring preferred themes beyond the standard buckets.

raw_wording: Nous abordons l’année 2026 avec un positionnement de portefeuille discipliné, ancré dans les perspectives stratégiques et affichant une diversification robuste entre régions et classes d’actifs.
why: Constructive but diversified equity stance.

raw_wording: Sur les marchés actions, nous anticipons un élargissement des performances, les prévisions de croissance bénéficiaire au niveau mondial augurant de gains significatifs, notamment sur les marchés émergents.
why: Emerging markets are a named equity beneficiary of broader market participation.

raw_wording: Sur les marchés obligataires, la dette émergente offre la meilleure valeur.
why: Fixed income preference is led by value in EM debt.

raw_wording: Sur les marchés obligataires, la dette émergente offre la meilleure valeur.
why: EM debt is explicitly called the best value segment.

raw_wording: La baisse des taux d’intérêt américains, l’affaiblissement du billet vert, ainsi que la trêve commerciale entre les Etats-Unis et la Chine sont autant de vents porteurs.
why: Weakening USD is part of the supportive asset backdrop.

raw_wording: Nous tablons sur une nouvelle progression de l’or ; notre allocation dédiée devrait continuer à apporter de la diversification.
why: Gold is expected to rise further and diversify portfolios.

raw_wording: Les actions liées aux matières premières, en particulier celles du secteur des matériaux, offrent à présent une meilleure valeur et devraient bénéficier des investissements infrastructurels et de l’élargissement des dépenses liées à l’IA en 2026.
why: Materials and AI-linked commodity exposure benefit from infrastructure and capex.

raw_wording: Parallèlement, l’IA transforme les flux de capitaux. Les investisseurs font déjà preuve d’un plus grand discernement quant à l’ampleur des dépenses, et la concentration du marché ne laisse que peu de place à la complaisance.
why: AI remains important, but the firm stresses selectivity and valuation discipline.

raw_wording: La santé devient un pilier stratégique de l’investissement durable. Les infrastructures de santé, les outils de diagnostic et les plateformes de santé digitale permettent une exposition évolutive tant à l’impact qu’à la croissance.
why: Healthcare is treated as a strategic, investable resilience theme.

raw_wording: L’essor des infrastructures d’IA accélère l’électrification, un thème d’investissement clé dans le cadre de la transition vers la durabilité.
why: Electrification and decarbonization are explicit structural themes.

raw_wording: En Europe, la capacité de l’Allemagne à mettre en œuvre ses projets d’investissement dans les infrastructures, la technologie et la défense sera déterminante. La Chine entend profiter de sa trêve commerciale avec les Etats-Unis pour renforcer son indépendance technologique.
why: Defense and technological sovereignty are framed as key strategic drivers.

raw_wording: L’eau devient un enjeu géopolitique et économique majeur, et les entreprises proposant des technologies d’analyse et de purification évolutives à bas coût sont susceptibles d’attirer des capitaux liés à l’action climatique.
why: Water and climate adaptation form an additional thematic opportunity.

raw_wording: Le contexte favorable devrait soutenir la croissance mondiale en 2026 et réduire les écarts creusés par l’économie en K.
why: Pictet keeps a constructive cross-market baseline for 2026.

raw_wording: Aux Etats-Unis, la croissance devrait s’accélérer en début d’année dans le sillage de la fin de la paralysie budgétaire et des promesses de soutien.
why: The US is still supported early in 2026 by fiscal follow-through.

raw_wording: En Europe, la reprise s’appuiera sur le vaste programme budgétaire de l’Allemagne et sur l’intégration économique qui doit renforcer la compétitivité dans l’énergie, la finance et les communications.
why: Europe is supported by German stimulus and single-market deepening.

raw_wording: Une accélération plus tardive est attendue en Chine. Le pays devrait tirer profit de l’atténuation des incertitudes commerciales et de la politique anti-involution menée par Pékin.
why: China is expected to improve later in the year with policy support.

raw_wording: Les technologies spatiales et militaires recèlent un potentiel de croissance important pour l’Europe, d’autant que les investissements allemands se concentrent sur les infrastructures et la défense.
why: Defense and strategic autonomy are explicit European growth themes.

raw_wording: L’Union européenne poursuit le processus d’intégration économique et a fixé à 2028 le délai d’achèvement du marché unique, afin de renforcer la compétitivité dans l’énergie, la finance et les communications.
why: European integration is treated as a structural theme in its own right.

raw_wording: Ce cadre demeure propice aux marchés d’actions, au crédit et, plus largement, aux actifs risqués, tout en justifiant davantage de prudence à l’égard des obligations souveraines.
why: Constructive on risk assets, with selectivity and sovereign caution.

raw_wording: À mesure que le cycle progresse, la sélectivité devient déterminante. Nous privilégions un portage défensif, reposant sur des émetteurs solides.
why: Fixed income is used for selective carry and defense.

raw_wording: La forte dispersion entre émetteurs, notations et secteurs permet de bâtir des portefeuilles générant un rendement moyen de 5% avec un profil investment grade assurant un équilibre efficace entre risque et rémunération attendue.
why: Prefers solid issuers with an investment-grade profile.

raw_wording: Les obligations à 30 ans émises par des pays aux finances publiques dégradées n’offrent pas une rémunération suffisante. Nous préférons les éviter, voire adopter des positions vendeuses sur ces dernières.
why: Long sovereign duration is explicitly avoided.

raw_wording: Les obligations à 30 ans émises par des pays aux finances publiques dégradées n’offrent pas une rémunération suffisante. Nous préférons les éviter, voire adopter des positions vendeuses sur ces dernières.
why: Nominal long-dated sovereign bonds are unattractive.

raw_wording: Ce contexte est favorable aux obligations indexées à l’inflation. Elles combinent ainsi portage, protection et convexité.
why: Inflation linkers are preferred for carry and protection.

raw_wording: Les couvertures les plus pertinentes se trouvent aujourd’hui dans les CDS, le yen japonais et l’or.
why: Yen is treated as a key hedge.

raw_wording: Le peso chilien, le rand sud-africain et le dollar australien se distinguent par l’amélioration des termes de l’échange, la discipline budgétaire ou l’exposition aux matières premières.
why: Commodity- and reform-supported FX are preferred.

raw_wording: Les couvertures les plus pertinentes se trouvent aujourd’hui dans les CDS, le yen japonais et l’or.
why: Gold is a named hedge.

raw_wording: L’IA et la technologie restent une tendance haussière alimentée par des forces structurelles, mais la principale inquiétude porte sur l’ampleur de l’engouement autour de ce thème très populaire.
why: Structural AI upside remains, but crowding and bubble risk are acknowledged.

raw_wording: Nous associons les leaders technologiques aux valeurs défensives des secteurs de la santé et de la consommation de base.
why: Healthcare is one half of the equity barbell.

raw_wording: La fin de la Pax Americana remet la souveraineté nationale au centre des priorités industrielles, entraînant des investissements majeurs dans la défense, les infrastructures et l’électrification.
why: Electrification is an explicit industrial theme.

raw_wording: La fin de la Pax Americana remet la souveraineté nationale au centre des priorités industrielles, entraînant des investissements majeurs dans la défense, les infrastructures et l’électrification.
why: Defense and sovereignty are central themes.

raw_wording: Nous associons les leaders technologiques aux valeurs défensives des secteurs de la santé et de la consommation de base.
why: Staples / defensive consumption is part of the barbell theme set.

raw_wording: Action for investors: within developed markets, remain focused on the US in terms of growth prospects.
why: EFG keeps the US as the preferred developed-market equity exposure.

raw_wording: In Europe the growth outlook is mixed. The lagged impact of lower European Central Bank rates will provide a tailwind to regional growth in 2026 and the German economy is expected to receive a significant boost from higher fiscal spending.
why: Europe has support from rates and fiscal policy, but the view is not cleanly bullish.

raw_wording: Emerging markets with strong domestic growth prospects should remain attractive for investors.
why: EFG remains constructive on selective EM equity exposure.

raw_wording: Despite structural headwinds, China is still set to be the predominant emerging economy. The rapid adoption of new technology, notably in green energy, artificial intelligence and electrification, remains an important strength.
why: China is still treated as the dominant EM growth and technology platform.

raw_wording: There remains value in shorter- to mid-term duration exposure. This environment allows investors to borrow at the short end of the yield curve and invest further out to capture attractive carry opportunities.
why: The bond stance is constructive through carry rather than deep duration risk.

raw_wording: There remains value in shorter- to mid-term duration exposure.
why: EFG prefers shorter-to-intermediate duration rather than long-duration risk.

raw_wording: There are several markets — notably France and the UK — for which fiscal sustainability will be questioned in 2026. Pledges to bring government finances under control may pacify the sharks for a while but when confidence evaporates bond markets will look vulnerable once again.
why: Government-bond exposure is constrained by fiscal-sustainability risk.

raw_wording: Look for opportunities in high-yielding emerging sovereign markets where there is the prospect of capital gains as long-term yields decline.
why: Local EM sovereign debt is one of the clearer preferred bond segments.

raw_wording: In a general environment of a stable-to-weakening US dollar, the risk of currency depreciation seems contained for 2026.
why: The base case assumes a softer dollar.

raw_wording: Technological advancement, most notably artificial intelligence, and the significant investment required to develop the data infrastructure of the future will be central issues. Key questions will include how these investments will be financed and what the long-term benefits will be.
why: AI is central, but EFG explicitly frames it with financing and payoff questions.

raw_wording: The rapid adoption of new technology, notably in green energy, artificial intelligence and electrification, remains an important strength.
why: EFG links EM and China strength to green-energy and electrification themes.

raw_wording: Continued rivalry between the US and China, the forging of new alliances and bifurcation are the key geopolitical themes we see for 2026.
why: Geopolitical fragmentation remains a core risk theme.

raw_wording: A weaker dollar, stretched equity valuations, and earnings concentrated in a narrow set of AI-linked tech firms add to the risk of US equity underperformance. Non-US equities appear better positioned for sustained outperformance.
why: Lazard expects the US to lose some of its exceptionalism.

raw_wording: We expect a brighter outlook for the Eurozone economy in 2026. Low interest rates and energy prices, rising real wages, and continued fiscal expansion should support growth.
why: Europe benefits from easing, lower energy costs, and fiscal support.

raw_wording: Emerging markets are particularly appealing, and they can offer AI exposure at far lower valuations. In many cases, local currency EM debt also stands out for appreciation potential.
why: Lazard sees stronger value and broader opportunity in EM than in crowded US markets.

raw_wording: Outside the United States, debt sustainability concerns limit the appeal of many other developed market bonds.
why: DM sovereign bonds are capped by debt-sustainability concerns.

raw_wording: In many cases, local currency EM debt stands out for appreciation potential.
why: EM local debt is singled out as a more attractive fixed-income segment.

raw_wording: A weaker dollar is likely, and with global investors increasingly seeking to hedge their US dollar exposure on any US currency rally, there are also potential gains on FX in addition to share prices.
why: Dollar weakness is part of the non-US outperformance thesis.

raw_wording: Meaningful market concerns are hard to find with the exception of gold, which has rallied sharply. I believe uncertainty surrounding Fed independence was a meaningful contributor, with the effects likely to be most visible via the US dollar and gold in the near term.
why: Gold is the explicit hedge against Fed-credibility risk and dollar stress.

raw_wording: I am skeptical that this AI boom will be sustained through 2026. Make no mistake: AI will be revolutionary for the global economy, but the rules of economics and finance still apply.
why: AI is structurally important, but Lazard questions the near-term capital cycle and payoff.

raw_wording: Defense-Driven Growth: if higher spending is sustained and the funds are used largely for military research, development, and production of military equipment in Europe, the multiplier effects could surprise on the upside.
why: European defense spending is treated as a real growth and investment theme.

raw_wording: Au tournant de l’année, nous maintenons notre position surpondérée en actions. Sans resserrement monétaire de la Fed ou récession, les investisseurs ne doivent pas sous-pondérer les actions.
why: The house view is explicitly overweight equities.

raw_wording: Europe remains robust. The eurozone is expected to continue growing steadily, and rising fiscal spending, particularly in Germany, is expected to provide further tailwinds in 2026.
why: Europe is supported by German fiscal stimulus and improving momentum.

raw_wording: We expect market breadth to pick up again next year. This would give many sectors that have been weak due to high interest rates the potential to catch up, especially small caps.
why: Small caps are named as catch-up beneficiaries of broader market breadth.

raw_wording: Overall, we are therefore cautiously positive about the corporate bond markets for 2026. Moderate confidence extends beyond government bonds.
why: Constructive on credit even while cautious on sovereigns.

raw_wording: Corporate bonds offer interesting returns, and more cautious investors may consider covered bonds with strong credit ratings and a yield advantage over high-quality government bonds.
why: Higher-quality credit is treated as attractive versus sovereign bonds.

raw_wording: The high-yield segment has seen structural improvements in terms of collateralisation and interest rate sensitivity. Here, we see selective opportunities.
why: High yield is supported, albeit selectively.

raw_wording: We expect yields on ten-year German government bonds to rise moderately and yields on US Treasuries to rise somewhat more sharply. Neither German nor American government bonds are likely to generate attractive returns.
why: The firm is negative on duration in core sovereign markets.

raw_wording: Neither German nor American government bonds are likely to generate attractive returns. For euro investors, safe government bonds remain unattractive across the board.
why: Government bonds are explicitly unattractive for euro investors.

raw_wording: Overall, we are cautiously positive about the corporate bond markets for 2026. In the credit segment, temporary corrections are opportunities to selectively increase risk exposure.
why: Corporate credit is the preferred bond area.

raw_wording: In bonds, we prefer covered bonds, high-yield bonds and niche segments such as local currency bonds from frontier markets or catastrophe bonds.
why: The preferred bond mix includes frontier and EM-style local currency debt.

raw_wording: The greenback will remain under pressure in the medium term. We therefore anticipate that the EUR/USD exchange rate will move toward the 1.20 handle in 2026.
why: Berenberg expects continued medium-term dollar weakness.

raw_wording: The precious metal is likely to remain an important component of portfolios in an environment of ongoing geopolitical uncertainty, increased inflation volatility, fiscal dominance and continued central bank purchases.
why: Gold remains a strategic portfolio component.

raw_wording: Incremental purchases from Europe and a recovery in demand from the US are likely to lead to a growing supply deficit for copper in 2026. The same applies to aluminium.
why: Industrial metals are supported by tightening supply-demand balances.

raw_wording: Artificial intelligence remains a global growth driver thanks to massive capital investments in data centres and energy infrastructure.
why: AI remains a central structural growth theme.

raw_wording: Massive investments in data centres and energy infrastructure will ensure that artificial intelligence remains a key growth driver.
why: Data-center and power infrastructure spending is a favored structural theme.

raw_wording: Fiscal stimulus will boost the German economy in 2026, with additional government spending particularly in the defence sector.
why: Defense spending is a core European support theme.

raw_wording: The global economy was shaken up, but there was no apparent breakage. Global growth remained slightly above 3%.
why: Oddo remains broadly constructive on risk assets in its central scenario.

raw_wording: Neither the tariff war, nor trade tensions, nor the legal and political noise brought the economy to its knees. The labor market weakened significantly, but AI spending continued to boom.
why: The U.S. remains resilient because AI spending offsets softer labor conditions.

raw_wording: The modest recovery is expected to continue in 2026, based on a decline in household savings rates and an acceleration in the German economy thanks to Merz’s stimulus plan.
why: Europe is improving, but the view remains cautious because the recovery is still modest.

raw_wording: The Chinese economy remains paradoxical. At the macro level, internal weaknesses are becoming more pronounced, yet in 2026 China is set to unveil a new five-year plan consolidating its ambitions for leadership.
why: China remains strategically ambitious but macro-fragile.

raw_wording: The financial sphere still faces the risk of an AI bubble bursting.
why: Oddo explicitly treats AI exuberance as a market weak point.

raw_wording: Multiple geopolitical tensions could weigh on market sentiment. Europe faces triple pressure—from the United States, Russia, and China.
why: Geopolitical pressure is a direct downside factor for Europe and markets.

raw_wording: After a year of announcements and preparations, the infrastructure and defense investment plan should take shape.
why: Infrastructure and defense are among the few explicit positive structural themes in the note.

raw_wording: En 2026, les marchés devront encore arbitrer entre promesses de croissance, fragilités persistantes et nécessité d’une gestion sélective.
why: Candriam is constructive but explicitly selective rather than broad-beta bullish.

raw_wording: Le plan ReArm Europe, la relance budgétaire allemande et l’assouplissement monétaire créeront un policy mix encore plus favorable en 2026.
why: Europe is supported by fiscal expansion, easier policy, and rearmament.

raw_wording: Pékin s’est imposée dans la bataille commerciale, mais la Chine peine encore à sortir de la déflation. Ses marchés ont rebondi, tirés par les valeurs technologiques et les soutiens publics.
why: China benefits from tech and policy support, but internal fragility remains.

raw_wording: La sélectivité demeure essentielle tant sur le marché du crédit public que sur le privé. Le segment du crédit privé pourrait se confronter à ses limites: moindre transparence, liquidité réduite et affaiblissement des protections contractuelles.
why: Candriam favors credit opportunity only with clear selectivity and stress-testing.

raw_wording: L’or, longtemps perçu comme une protection, s’apparente désormais davantage à un actif spéculatif.
why: Gold is no longer treated as a clean hedge.

raw_wording: L’ère de l’intelligence artificielle: entre révolution et illusion. Une nouvelle révolution industrielle est en marche, mais les valorisations interrogent et l’euphorie actuelle n’est pas sans rappeler la bulle des années 2000.
why: AI is transformative, but Candriam is explicit about valuation sensitivity and bubble risk.

raw_wording: Le secteur de la santé sera l’un des grands bénéficiaires de cette transformation. Avec des valorisations raisonnables et un regain d’activité en fusions-acquisitions, la santé pourrait redevenir un pilier de performance à long terme.
why: Healthcare is one of Candriam’s clearest preferred themes.

raw_wording: L’essor de l’IA s’accompagne d’un défi énergétique colossal. Répondre à cette demande impose d’accélérer la transition énergétique: sans énergie verte et abondante, pas de révolution durable.
why: Energy transition is explicitly linked to enabling AI at scale.

raw_wording: Les chaînes de valeur se redessinent au nom de la sécurité et de la souveraineté. Le plan ReArm Europe et la recomposition des alliances créent un nouveau cadre d’investissement.
why: Security, sovereignty, and rearmament are treated as durable structural themes.

raw_wording: L’intégration de tendances structurelles — intelligence artificielle, santé, transition énergétique, investissement à impact — renforce la robustesse des portefeuilles.
why: Impact and resilience are explicit portfolio-building themes beyond the base grid.

raw_wording: United States: We are overweight. Strong corporate earnings, driven in part by the AI theme, are supported by a favorable macro backdrop and continued Federal Reserve easing.
why: BlackRock remains explicitly overweight U.S. equities.

raw_wording: Europe: We stay selective, favoring financials, utilities and healthcare. We are neutral.
why: Europe is investable, but not a broad overweight.

raw_wording: Japanese stocks are one of our preferred tactical and strategic exposures. We are overweight.
why: Japan is one of BlackRock’s clearest preferred international equity markets.

raw_wording: Within equities, we favor EM over DM yet get selective in both. We see opportunities across EM linked to AI and the energy transition.
why: EM is explicitly preferred over developed markets.

raw_wording: China: We are neutral. Trade relations with the U.S. have steadied, but property stress and an aging population still constrain the macro outlook. We still favor China tech within our neutral view.
why: China is not a full positive call, but BlackRock still supports China tech within a neutral country stance.

raw_wording: We turn underweight long-term Treasuries.
why: BlackRock remains cautious on long-duration U.S. sovereign exposure.

raw_wording: EM hard currency: We are overweight. A weaker U.S. dollar, lower U.S. rates and effective EM fiscal and monetary policy have improved economic resilience. We prefer high yield bonds.
why: EM hard-currency debt is explicitly overweighted.

raw_wording: A weaker U.S. dollar has boosted local currency EM debt, though it is unclear if this weakening will persist.
why: The base case still embeds a softer dollar backdrop.

raw_wording: We like gold as a tactical play with idiosyncratic drivers but do not see it as a long-term portfolio hedge.
why: Gold is still a positive tactical allocation even if not a strategic anchor.

raw_wording: We remain pro-risk and see the AI theme still the main driver of U.S. equities. We stay risk-on and overweight U.S. stocks on the AI theme.
why: AI is still BlackRock’s dominant equity theme.

raw_wording: The next phase of the AI buildout may be more about energy and resolving bottlenecks. We see opportunities across EM linked to AI and the energy transition.
why: BlackRock explicitly ties AI deployment to energy and infrastructure demand.

raw_wording: Fragmentation: AI and defense. We see India as a potential beneficiary of heightened geopolitical fragmentation that benefits multi-aligned connector countries.
why: Defense and fragmentation are treated as investable structural forces.

raw_wording: La diversification reste la meilleure défense dans un monde où les marchés actions sont concentrés et les valorisations élevées. Il convient de rééquilibrer les portefeuilles entre les styles, les secteurs, les tailles et les régions.
why: Constructive equity stance, but diversified beyond concentrated winners.

raw_wording: Il convient de rééquilibrer les portefeuilles afin de saisir les opportunités, notamment sur les marchés émergents et les actifs européens.
why: European assets are explicitly highlighted in the diversification push.

raw_wording: Il convient de rééquilibrer les portefeuilles afin de saisir les opportunités, notamment sur les marchés émergents et les actifs européens.
why: Emerging markets are highlighted as part of the preferred diversification set.

raw_wording: La dynamique européenne se maintient. Cela peut créer des opportunités dans le crédit d’entreprise en euro, les petites et moyennes capitalisations et les thèmes liés à la défense, aux infrastructures et à l’autonomie stratégique.
why: European SMID exposure is explicitly favored.

raw_wording: Dans ce nouveau paradigme, nous privilégions les obligations européennes et, aux États-Unis, le crédit investment grade par rapport aux bons du Trésor américain plus volatils.
why: Investment grade is preferred over more volatile US Treasuries.

raw_wording: La dynamique européenne se maintient. Cela peut créer des opportunités dans le crédit d’entreprise en euro.
why: Euro corporate credit is an explicit opportunity.

raw_wording: Les obligations émergentes se distinguent pour leur revenu élevé et leur potentiel de diversification, tandis que les actions émergentes offrent un éventail diversifié d’opportunités.
why: EM bonds are favored for income and diversification.

raw_wording: Nous continuons de nous positionner pour un dollar faible, bien que non linéaire, en 2026.
why: Weak USD is an explicit FX position.

raw_wording: Les devises émergentes pourraient s’apprécier dans un contexte de dépréciation du dollar américain. Nous privilégions les devises à portage élevé en Amérique latine, dans la région CEEMEA et en Asie.
why: Long EM FX as the dollar weakens.

raw_wording: Cela nécessite une prise de risque sélective et une diversification accrue dans les devises, l’or et les actifs réels.
why: Gold is part of the recommended diversification toolkit.

raw_wording: Les thèmes de l’IA et de la transformation technologique stimulent la demande énergétique dans un contexte de transition vers la neutralité carbone.
why: AI remains a major structural growth and capex theme.

raw_wording: Les thèmes de l’IA et de la transformation technologique stimulent la demande énergétique dans un contexte de transition vers la neutralité carbone. Cela ouvre des opportunités parmi les actions liées au thème climatique, les infrastructures durables et les obligations vertes.
why: Energy transition and climate infrastructure are explicit opportunities.

raw_wording: Nous sommes confrontés à une ère de désordre contrôlé : un monde multipolaire remodelé par une révolution technologique et des divergences budgétaires.
why: Multipolar disorder is treated as a structural backdrop and source of opportunity.

raw_wording: La dynamique européenne se maintient. Cela peut créer des opportunités dans les thèmes liés à la défense, aux infrastructures et à l’autonomie stratégique.
why: Defense and strategic autonomy are named investment themes.

raw_wording: Les actions mondiales pourraient bien clôturer l’année sur une progression à deux chiffres.
why: JPM starts from a broadly constructive global equity backdrop.

raw_wording: Selon nous, l’économie américaine devrait profiter des effets positifs des gains de pouvoir d’achat, des baisses de taux et des investissements continus dans l’intelligence artificielle.
why: The U.S. remains supported by fiscal liquidity, easing, and AI investment.

raw_wording: L’activité devrait s’accélérer de manière significative en Europe continentale en 2026. L’énorme plan de relance budgétaire annoncé en Allemagne commencera à se répercuter dans les données économiques.
why: Europe is supported by German fiscal expansion and easier policy.

raw_wording: La reprise des valeurs technologiques et les soutiens publics ont soutenu l’ensemble du marché chinois et devraient contribuer à améliorer la confiance des consommateurs.
why: China improves at the margin through tech leadership and policy support.

raw_wording: Pour se prémunir contre un décrochage des actions technologiques, les investisseurs devraient envisager des obligations de haute qualité à long terme.
why: JPM explicitly reintroduces long high-quality bonds as protection.

raw_wording: Les investisseurs devraient envisager des obligations de haute qualité à long terme pour se protéger contre un décrochage des actions technologiques.
why: High-quality sovereign-like duration regains a defensive role.

raw_wording: Si l’indépendance de la banque centrale américaine devait réellement être remise en question, le dollar serait probablement l’actif qui en pâtirait le plus. Le dollar américain continue de chuter face à un large panier de devises.
why: JPM explicitly flags downside dollar risk.

raw_wording: Un autre actif à considérer est l’or. Dans les périodes d’inflation chronique, ses propriétés de réserve de valeur en font un important atout de diversification de portefeuille.
why: Gold is a recommended hedge against chronic inflation and credibility risk.

raw_wording: Risque baissier: les actions technologiques faiblissent. L’engouement pour l’IA s’affaiblit si la demande en infrastructures a été surestimée, mais notre scénario central reste soutenu par des investissements continus dans l’IA.
why: AI remains a growth engine, but JPM explicitly maps a downside bubble scenario.

raw_wording: Les tensions géopolitiques contribuent à la une des journaux et demeurent un risque potentiel de déraillement important pour les investisseurs.
why: Geopolitical tension remains one of the key downside scenarios.

raw_wording: Les autres pays européens ont décidé d’augmenter leur budget de défense. Les dépenses de défense peuvent améliorer la productivité si la recherche et développement débouche sur de l’innovation.
why: European defense spending is framed as both macro support and structural investment theme.

raw_wording: Compte tenu du contexte actuel, il nous semble essentiel de diversifier au maximum l’exposition aux actions, d’adopter une exposition active, d’investir à l’échelle mondiale et de gérer efficacement les risques.
why: Constructive on equities, but through active global diversification rather than passive beta.

raw_wording: En Europe, la priorité accordée à la sécurité nationale et économique se traduit par une augmentation des investissements dans les infrastructures et les capacités de défense.
why: European equities benefit from capex in defense and infrastructure.

raw_wording: Sur les segments des petites et moyennes capitalisations, nous pensons que les facilitateurs, fournissant les pioches et les pelles du boom de l’IA, offrent d’excellentes opportunités.
why: Small and mid caps linked to AI infrastructure are explicitly attractive.

raw_wording: Les cycles d’assouplissement offrent selon nous des opportunités dans toutes les classes d’actifs. Les baisses de taux pourraient profiter aux instruments obligataires, notamment les bons du Trésor américain à court terme et les obligations Investment grade.
why: Easing cycles support a constructive fixed-income stance.

raw_wording: Les baisses de taux pourraient profiter aux instruments obligataires, notamment les bons du Trésor américain à court terme et les obligations Investment grade, dont la composante taux est plus élevée que par le passé.
why: Investment-grade bonds are a primary beneficiary of rate cuts.

raw_wording: Certains segments obligataires pourraient être intéressants, notamment les titres à haut rendement et le crédit titrisé, qui peuvent générer un revenu non-négligeable.
why: High yield is presented as an income-generating opportunity.

raw_wording: Les baisses de taux pourraient profiter aux bons du Trésor américain à court terme.
why: The preferred sovereign duration exposure is on the short end.

raw_wording: Les baisses de taux pourraient profiter aux bons du Trésor américain à court terme.
why: Short-term Treasuries are one of the named bond opportunities.

raw_wording: Le crédit américain IG évolue en milieu de cycle, et non en fin de cycle. Le marché à haut rendement se porte également bien, avec une amélioration de la qualité de crédit et une duration plus courte.
why: Corporate credit is supported by resilient fundamentals.

raw_wording: Les obligations en devises locales et la dette extérieure des pays émergents devraient également bénéficier de l’assouplissement de la Fed.
why: EM debt is a direct beneficiary of easier Fed policy.

raw_wording: Sur le front des devises, le dollar américain a enregistré des performances mitigées sur fond de baisse des taux de la Fed. Historiquement, le dollar a tendance à se raffermir ou à évoluer en dents de scie lorsque la baisse des taux de la Fed n’entraîne pas de récession.
why: Goldman does not have a clean one-way bearish dollar view.

raw_wording: L’univers d’investissement lié à l’IA devrait s’élargir et donner accès à de nouvelles opportunités. Mais la visibilité sur la rentabilité de ces investissements reste faible, ce qui rend essentielle une analyse rigoureuse des fondamentaux.
why: AI remains fertile, but the firm stresses valuation and monetization discipline.

raw_wording: La demande de données, alimentée par l’IA, va entraîner une accélération massive de la croissance de la demande mondiale en électricité. Les infrastructures, les obligations vertes et le financement de l’énergie durable offriront selon nous des opportunités prometteuses.
why: Power, infrastructure, and sustainable energy capex are explicit opportunity areas.

raw_wording: Les tensions géopolitiques, les engagements de l’OTAN en matière de défense et la nouvelle dynamique des projets de réindustrialisation imposent aux nations et aux entreprises de construire des chaînes d’approvisionnement résilientes et de sécuriser leurs ressources critiques.
why: Geopolitics is treated as both a risk source and an investment catalyst.

raw_wording: Nous pensons que le thème de la sécurité économique va s’imposer en 2026. Les gérants actifs auront de nombreuses opportunités d’investir dans des entreprises idéalement positionnées pour profiter de cette transition.
why: Economic security, defense, and national resilience are core themes.

raw_wording: Le thème de la sécurité économique va occuper une place centrale, avec des besoins d’investissement gigantesques dans la résilience des chaînes d’approvisionnement, les ressources critiques et la cybersécurité.
why: Supply-chain resilience and economic security are additional thematic calls.

raw_wording: We expect global equities to rise by around 15% by the end of 2026, and we think investors underallocated to stocks should add to equities.
why: Explicit call to add to equities.

raw_wording: Our positive views on US tech and US equities are core drivers, and in the US we favor technology, utilities, and health care.
why: US remains the core equity engine.

raw_wording: We upgrade European equities to Attractive, with a particular focus on the Eurozone.
why: Europe is explicitly upgraded to Attractive.

raw_wording: Beyond the US, we also see opportunities in Europe, Japan, and China. Strong liquidity, earnings, and retail flows should sustain momentum for Chinese, Asian, and emerging market equities.
why: Emerging market equities are part of the preferred opportunity set.

raw_wording: In Asia, we favor China, and China’s tech sector stands out as a top global opportunity.
why: China is singled out within Asia as a preferred equity market.

raw_wording: A robust fixed income allocation provides stability and income while managing risk.
why: Rates coming down shift the focus back to portfolio income.

raw_wording: We recommend diversifying across developed market government bonds, corporate and emerging market bonds, with quality bonds offering attractive yields.
why: Quality / higher-grade bonds are treated as core portfolio ballast.

raw_wording: We believe an average duration of five to seven years balances risk and return for long-term investors, especially when rates are expected to change gradually.
why: Prefers intermediate duration rather than an extreme duration stance.

raw_wording: We recommend diversifying across developed market government bonds for stability.
why: Government bonds are held for stability.

raw_wording: We recommend diversifying across corporate bonds for higher income.
why: Corporate bonds are part of the income allocation.

raw_wording: We recommend diversifying across emerging market bonds for higher income.
why: EM bonds are part of the diversified income mix.

raw_wording: Currency strategy matters. We favor the euro and Australian dollar over the US dollar, as US rate cuts may weigh on the greenback.
why: US dollar is the funding currency they prefer against.

raw_wording: Currency strategy matters. We favor the euro and Australian dollar over the US dollar.
why: Euro is a preferred currency versus the dollar.

raw_wording: Gold has historically been an effective portfolio hedge during market stress, inflation fears, or geopolitical uncertainty. We believe a modest allocation can enhance diversification and buffer against systemic risks.
why: Gold is explicitly recommended as a hedge allocation.

raw_wording: AI and technology have been key drivers of global equity markets. Strong capex and adoption should fuel further gains in 2026.
why: AI remains a core growth engine.

raw_wording: In the US, we favor technology, utilities, and health care.
why: Healthcare is one of the explicitly preferred sectors.

raw_wording: Commodities are supported by long-term trends like the global energy transition. Within the asset class, we see particular opportunities in copper, aluminum, and agricultural commodities.
why: Energy transition and power/resource demand are explicit structural drivers.

raw_wording: Gold has historically been an effective portfolio hedge during market stress, inflation fears, or geopolitical uncertainty.
why: Geopolitical risk is a central hedge rationale rather than a pure growth theme.

raw_wording: Opportunities span AI’s enabling, intelligence, and application layers, as well as power and resources and longevity.
why: Longevity is a named structural theme beyond AI.

raw_wording: Europe, China and India offer diversified, attractively priced equity markets, alongside significant policy support. Together, these three regions form a compelling equity allocation framework for navigating the shifting global order.
why: Overall equity positioning favors a diversified ex-US opportunity set.

raw_wording: We currently view US equities as richly priced. Combined with near record levels of concentration, a selective investment approach is required.
why: US equities are treated cautiously because of valuation and concentration risk.

raw_wording: We believe European equities are, across sectors, currently more attractively priced than many US counterparts, giving them appeal in terms of both value and growth.
why: Europe is preferred on valuation and breadth grounds.

raw_wording: In Asia, India currently stands out as a market with high potential due to multiple tailwinds and a broader, more diversified set of investment opportunities.
why: Emerging-market equity exposure is supported through India-led diversification.

raw_wording: China’s equity market remains deep and attractively priced, as well as under-owned by foreign investors, presenting contrarian opportunities for long-term capital flows.
why: China is framed as a contrarian equity opportunity with policy support.

raw_wording: This growth and inflation combination underpins an overall benign environment for fixed income and creates opportunities for selective duration and credit positioning across regions.
why: The fixed-income backdrop is described as benign rather than defensive only.

raw_wording: Developed markets duration offers resilience, while emerging market debt can provide yield enhancement and diversification.
why: EM debt is explicitly named as a preferred diversifier and yield source.

raw_wording: A potentially weaker dollar may prompt global investors to rethink high exposures to US assets.
why: The dollar is treated as a headwind to concentrated US exposure.

raw_wording: The Japanese yen could further consolidate its safe-haven status, supported by political stability and the Bank of Japan’s shift in response to higher core inflation.
why: JPY is framed as a scalable safe-haven diversifier.

raw_wording: Gold remains a key diversification tool for us, though it may have reached near-term peak levels. We continue to hold positions.
why: Gold remains a core hedge even after strong performance.

raw_wording: The US and China continue to lead the AI revolution, with spillovers to other regions. A broadening of tech spend outside the US could sustain growth and usher in a truly global AI revolution.
why: AI is treated as a broad structural growth theme rather than a narrow US trade.

raw_wording: Funding the energy transition and digital infrastructure will create opportunities across asset classes, including for investors with the ability to hold long-term and illiquid assets.
why: Energy transition is a named multi-asset opportunity set.

raw_wording: Geopolitical risks remain elevated, particularly around Russia and East Asia.
why: Geopolitics is presented primarily as an elevated cross-asset risk.

raw_wording: In its quest for increased strategic autonomy, Europe is embracing a more expansive fiscal policy and significant spending initiatives, especially in Germany, on defense and military hardware.
why: European strategic autonomy and defense spending are explicit investable themes.

raw_wording: Digital infrastructure. Data centres, fibre networks and 5G connectivity form the backbone of Europe’s digital sovereignty. We continue to see opportunities, both within equity and debt, going into 2026.
why: Digital infrastructure is a separate structural theme beyond the standard buckets.

raw_wording: We remain optimistic and expect solid returns in the vast majority of asset classes in the coming twelve months. So long as the economic cycle remains intact, the positive momentum in equity markets should, in our view, continue.
why: DWS remains constructive on the overall equity backdrop.

raw_wording: Because of the uncertainty attached to AI, we are favoring the less technology-heavy European and Japanese equity markets over the U.S. market.
why: The US is relatively de-emphasized because of concentration and AI-related uncertainty.

raw_wording: Because of the uncertainty attached to AI, we are favoring the less technology-heavy European and Japanese equity markets over the U.S. market.
why: Europe is preferred as a less concentrated alternative to the US.

raw_wording: Because of the uncertainty attached to AI, we are favoring the less technology-heavy European and Japanese equity markets over the U.S. market.
why: Japan is preferred for the same diversification and valuation reasons as Europe.

raw_wording: Corporate bonds remain attractive in our view, despite very low credit spreads, thanks to the supportive macro environment and high demand.
why: The fixed-income preference is constructive through credit rather than sovereign duration.

raw_wording: Corporate bonds remain attractive in our view, though we favor a selective approach in the high-yield segment.
why: High yield is investable, but only selectively.

raw_wording: Corporate bonds remain attractive in our view, despite very low credit spreads, thanks to the supportive macro environment and high demand.
why: Corporate bonds are the clearest preferred bond segment in the note.

raw_wording: In our view, the U.S. dollar has partially lost its status as a safe haven and is likely to weaken over time.
why: DWS explicitly expects dollar weakness.

raw_wording: Geopolitics could weigh on equity markets, but it should boost gold and oil.
why: Gold is a direct beneficiary of geopolitical hedging demand.

raw_wording: Geopolitics could weigh on equity markets, but it should boost gold and oil. For oil, however, we currently continue to anticipate structural oversupply.
why: Oil has geopolitical upside but a weaker structural supply-demand backdrop.

raw_wording: We also have a constructive view of the AI boom, though AI’s ability to surprise, negatively or positively, was once again demonstrated at the start of the year.
why: AI is supported, but DWS explicitly treats it as two-sided.

raw_wording: We see three major risks: AI, sovereign debt sustainability and geopolitics.
why: Geopolitics is explicitly identified as a major portfolio risk.

raw_wording: We see three major risks: AI, sovereign debt sustainability and geopolitics.
why: Sovereign debt sustainability is a named macro risk theme.

raw_wording: European equities offer compelling value, particularly as the region pursues greater strategic autonomy, potentially offering a broadening set of opportunities for forward-looking investors.
why: Europe is explicitly highlighted as the better value market.

raw_wording: Low US Treasury yields, and a weaker US dollar, are typically positive factors for emerging market equities.
why: EM equities benefit from the rates and dollar backdrop.

raw_wording: Of the three countries with the largest technology sectors — South Korea, Taiwan, and China — the latter offers greater diversity. We believe China’s technology sector has the additional advantage of being somewhat protected from current trade tensions.
why: China is preferred within EM tech because of breadth and relative insulation.

raw_wording: Fixed income markets should benefit from continued central bank easing in 2026. The core scenario is positive for credit markets notwithstanding tight credit spreads and signs of increased leverage.
why: The overall bond stance is constructive, especially for credit.

raw_wording: We believe opportunities exist across defensive sectors, real estate, and high yield, but a nimble approach is required.
why: High yield is investable, but only with explicit selectivity.

raw_wording: Despite this benign rate outlook, sovereign markets will remain at risk of increased investor concern on the fiscal side.
why: Government bonds are constrained by fiscal-risk concerns.

raw_wording: Prevailing yields in credit markets are attractive and should deliver attractive income-driven total returns.
why: Corporate credit is preferred for carry and income.

raw_wording: High yield and emerging market bonds continue to be interesting from a total return perspective.
why: EM debt remains one of the more attractive total-return bond segments.

raw_wording: Low US Treasury yields, and a weaker US dollar, are typically positive factors for emerging market equities.
why: BNP assumes a softer dollar backdrop in its EM allocation logic.

raw_wording: AI’s transformative potential persists, with robust fundamentals supporting valuations and innovation driving new opportunities across sectors.
why: AI remains a structural earnings and innovation theme.

raw_wording: The technological and societal shifts underlying the transition will persist and continue to demand investors’ attention. The Asia-Pacific region is a leader in driving the global energy transition, creating potentially significant investment opportunities.
why: Energy transition is treated as a durable investment theme despite policy noise.

raw_wording: Europe now allocates billions annually to defence, industrial resilience, and key technology sectors. We believe this is a structural trend that is here to stay.
why: Defense and strategic autonomy are presented as long-duration investment themes.

raw_wording: Sustainability remains a central focus, with European and Asian investors leading the charge on green bonds, decarbonisation, and climate solutions.
why: Sustainability and climate solutions are explicit structural themes beyond the core taxonomy.

raw_wording: Slight OW equities. Easy financial conditions and steady GDP growth ahead are fundamentally supportive.
why: The house view is explicitly overweight equities.

raw_wording: Over longer horizons, the US market appears overvalued compared to history. We recommend diversifying towards cheaper ex-US markets.
why: US equities are less preferred on valuation grounds.

raw_wording: OW EMU versus US, Switzerland, DAX, and MDAX.
why: Europe is preferred over the US in the regional equity mix.

raw_wording: OW EM, still less expensive on PEG and with low positioning: Korea, Poland, China, and India.
why: Emerging markets are explicitly overweighted.

raw_wording: We suggest diversifying M7 exposure into AI beneficiaries and users, big China IT names, electrification and global nuclear.
why: China tech is a preferred diversifier within the equity allocation.

raw_wording: Our preference remains for Credit and Equities over Govies and Cash.
why: The asset-allocation mix clearly favors credit over sovereign safety.

raw_wording: EA IG corporate bonds are preferred for their attractive carry and risk-return profile, especially compared to peripheral government bonds.
why: Investment-grade euro credit is a preferred fixed-income sleeve.

raw_wording: HY credit and equities are still favoured, supported by resilient but unspectacular growth, easing inflation, and continued monetary policy support.
why: High yield remains preferred in the current carry environment.

raw_wording: Government bonds are generally underweighted, with a moderately short duration recommended for euro area fixed income, as economic recovery and supply factors point to higher yields.
why: Duration is kept short in euro fixed income.

raw_wording: Government bonds are generally underweighted.
why: Sovereign duration is not the preferred bond exposure.

raw_wording: Tech issuance will boost supply, putting a floor under IG spreads, but carry is attractive. We continue to prefer EUR Credit over US.
why: Corporate credit, especially euro credit, is preferred for carry.

raw_wording: We keep our preference for EM sovereign debt, as it continues to offer attractive relative valuations and positive return potential, with a small tilt to high yield names.
why: EM sovereign debt remains a preferred yield segment.

raw_wording: The USD remains fundamentally dear, and the persistently high twin deficits are posing a lasting burden. We expect the USD to trend weaker over 2026.
why: Generali expects broad dollar depreciation.

raw_wording: The EUR has scope to catch up with economic green shoots in the euro area. Overall, we expect the EUR/USD to trend somewhat higher.
why: The euro is expected to appreciate as euro-area data improves.

raw_wording: JPY remains the most undervalued G10 currency and we expect the JPY to recover from its deep undervaluation in 2026.
why: JPY is seen as undervalued with recovery potential.

raw_wording: EM FXs will appreciate further, and we favour high yielders.
why: EM currencies are expected to strengthen further.

raw_wording: A mania may be developing, but it is too early to panic and underweight equities. We like to diversify into AI users, China Tech, or the electrification theme.
why: AI remains investable, but Generali explicitly flags concentration and competition risk.

raw_wording: The technological diffusion to the broader economy will support profitability, with sectors like Financials, BioTech or even retail well positioned to benefit.
why: Healthcare and biotech are named as AI-adoption beneficiaries.

raw_wording: We like to diversify into AI users, China Tech, or the electrification theme.
why: Electrification is a named preferred structural theme.

raw_wording: Overweight Financials, Defence and Software, along with Pharma, Retail, and Construction.
why: Defense is explicitly one of the overweight European sector calls.

raw_wording: Equities remain at the core of our strategy for 2026. The global backdrop supports this constructive view, consolidating equities as the main growth engine in diversified portfolios.
why: Equities are explicitly the core of the strategy.

why: Eurozone equities are supported by strong earnings expectations.

why: EM and LatAm are explicit parts of the positive equity mix.

raw_wording: The path toward neutral policy rates progresses at different speeds across geographies. In the Eurozone, strong corporate fundamentals and historically attractive carry make credit a stable source of return for 2026.
why: Santander sees carry and normalization as fixed-income opportunities.

raw_wording: We maintain a positive view on Eurozone investment-grade credit for 2026. Yields remain attractive and offer an additional premium over sovereign debt.
why: Eurozone IG is explicitly preferred.

why: Selective sovereign opportunities are part of the bond allocation.

why: Corporate credit is a stable return source in the Eurozone.

raw_wording: Nominal and inflation-linked UK government bonds still offer the highest yields among G7 economies.
why: Santander explicitly includes inflation-linked sovereign exposure in the opportunity set.

raw_wording: We remain positive on gold. Gold remains on an upward trend and continues to be the preferred hedge against geopolitical and inflation risks.
why: Gold is the preferred hedge in the commodity book.

source_file: Santander AM -Market Outlook 2026 Climbing towards new highs.pdf
raw_wording: In oil, we anticipate an oversupplied market in 2026, with Brent prices in the 55–60 USD per barrel range.
why: Oil is capped by oversupply in the base case.

source_file: Santander AM -Market Outlook 2026 Climbing towards new highs.pdf
raw_wording: Demand linked to the energy transition and infrastructure investment should remain supportive, but rising supply and slower growth in China will likely cap the recovery potential of industrial metals.
why: Supportive demand exists, but the upside is capped by supply and China.

source_file: Santander AM -Market Outlook 2026 Climbing towards new highs.pdf
raw_wording: Corporate investment in AI infrastructure, semiconductors, and the cloud is surging to record levels and is now large enough to contribute nearly half of U.S. real GDP growth.
why: AI capex is central to the house thematic framework.

raw_wording: Demand linked to the energy transition and infrastructure investment should remain supportive for industrial metals.
why: Energy transition and infrastructure remain supportive thematic drivers.

source_file: Santander AM -Market Outlook 2026 Climbing towards new highs.pdf
raw_wording: Trade, technology, and capital are being redirected by industrial policy, tariffs, and sanctions, fragmenting global supply chains.
why: Geopolitics is framed as a live risk that can reprice regions and asset classes.

raw_wording: Le prospettive per i mercati azionari globali restano positive: ci aspettiamo che gli indici principali registrino nuovi massimi, principalmente con il traino della crescita degli utili.
why: Anima remains constructive on global equities overall.

raw_wording: Privilegiamo gli Stati Uniti rispetto agli altri Paesi sviluppati.
why: The US remains the preferred developed-market equity exposure.

raw_wording: Pensiamo che anche Europa e Giappone registreranno performance positive, con il sostegno dei piani di stimolo fiscale locali.
why: Europe is supported by local fiscal stimulus and still expected to perform positively.

raw_wording: Pensiamo che anche Europa e Giappone registreranno performance positive, con il sostegno dei piani di stimolo fiscale locali.
why: Japan is also expected to benefit from local fiscal support.

raw_wording: Manteniamo un sovrappeso sull’Asia emergente, sostenuta dall’esposizione alla tecnologia e alle catene del valore legate all’intelligenza artificiale, nonché dal probabile indebolimento del dollaro.
why: Anima is explicitly overweight emerging Asia.

raw_wording: Privilegiamo i comparti ciclici rispetto ai difensivi, con un bias più costruttivo sui titoli Growth.
why: Growth remains the preferred style tilt.

raw_wording: Corporate Euro Investment Grade: restiamo investiti, ma manteniamo un approccio cauto, privilegiando i segmenti di mercato in cui i rischi sono meglio remunerati.
why: Euro IG remains a core credit exposure even with tighter valuations.

raw_wording: Il credito High Yield in euro ha registrato dinamiche peculiari. Gli sviluppi restano selettivamente favorevoli, ma i rischi paiono asimmetrici e non adeguatamente remunerati.
why: High yield is tactical and selective rather than a broad overweight.

raw_wording: Il 2025 è stato un altro anno positivo per il mondo del credito. I trend macro e di politica monetaria sono rimasti favorevoli e permane l’impostazione nella calibrazione dell’esposizione al rischio credito/tasso.
why: Corporate credit remains supported by resilient macro and easing policy.

raw_wording: Manteniamo un sovrappeso sull’Asia emergente, sostenuta anche dal probabile indebolimento del dollaro.
why: A weaker dollar is part of the EM preference.

raw_wording: Il rally dell’oro è stato sostenuto dalle persistenti tensioni geopolitiche, dall’incertezza sul contesto macroeconomico e politico, dalla debolezza del dollaro e dalle attese di allentamento monetario negli Stati Uniti.
why: Gold remains supported as a macro and geopolitical hedge.

raw_wording: Suggeriamo di aumentare l’esposizione ai settori legati alla tecnologia e a quelli più direttamente esposti allo sviluppo dell’intelligenza artificiale, che costituisce una delle principali leve di crescita strutturale per i mercati emergenti.
why: AI is treated as a core structural growth driver.

raw_wording: Le persistenti tensioni geopolitiche restano uno dei principali fattori a supporto dell’oro e di volatilità sui mercati.
why: Geopolitical tension remains a live portfolio risk factor.

raw_wording: Policy volatility, trade frictions and concentration risk have prompted some institutional investors to consider diversifying away from an overweight US allocation.
why: M&G explicitly argues against maintaining a concentrated US overweight.

raw_wording: Europe’s structural reforms are reinforcing its appeal as a region combining stability with growth potential.
why: Europe is treated as a better-balanced equity region.

raw_wording: Investors may seek to diversify beyond expensive AI stocks and consider more attractively valued stocks in regions such as Europe and emerging markets.
why: Emerging markets are part of the valuation-driven diversification trade.

raw_wording: Longer duration bonds now offer a more attractive pick up versus cash.
why: M&G sees more value in extending duration than earlier in the cycle.

raw_wording: The real (inflation-adjusted) yields available on long-dated government bonds are more attractive than they have been in many years.
why: Government bonds regain appeal through attractive real yields.

raw_wording: Gold, while lacking cash flow, could continue to serve as tail risk insurance in an environment of reduced dollar exposure and rising central bank demand.
why: Gold remains a preferred hedge asset.

raw_wording: We are only at the very start of the widespread application of AI, but this is the time to let go of passive exposure to AI stocks and take a more active investment approach.
why: AI is important, but M&G is explicitly cautious on passive exposure and valuation euphoria.

raw_wording: The most compelling opportunities sit within transformative sectors such as climate tech, fintech, cybersecurity and health tech.
why: Health tech is named as one of the most compelling structural themes.

raw_wording: The most compelling opportunities sit within sectors innovating within energy transition and leading environmental and societal change.
why: Energy transition remains a preferred long-duration theme.

raw_wording: Extreme volatility was all too often the prevailing backdrop: the Liberation Day tariffs crash, ongoing geopolitical conflict, a US credit rating downgrade and increasing questions about AI-led market momentum.
why: Geopolitics remains a live source of volatility and risk.

raw_wording: Capital is moving toward certainty and resilience, favouring assets that can withstand volatility and deliver sustainable growth.
why: Resilience is a recurring cross-asset theme in M&G’s outlook.
